answer:
The first matrix \(\begin{pmatrix}3 & 1 \\ 1 & 3\end{pmatrix}\) has eigenvalues \(4\) and \(2\).  
The second matrix \(\begin{pmatrix}2 & 2 & 1 \\ 1 & 3 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & 2\end{pmatrix}\) has eigenvalues \(1, 2, 3\).  
The third matrix \(\begin{pmatrix}2 & -1 & 0 \\ 9 & 4 & 6 \\ -8 & 0 & -3\end{pmatrix}\) has eigenvalues \(1, -1, 3\).  

These match option **C**.

\boxed{C}
